song.title: First Session
song.keywords: therapy, mental health, seeking help, first session, The Unseen Chorus
song.description: Full lyrics to 'First Session' from The Unseen Chorus (A Gay Musical) by Agust Islandia (Agust Smari Bjarkarson).

og.title: First Session Lyrics - The Unseen Chorus Musical
og.description: Track 19 from The Unseen Chorus. A song about taking the first courageous step into therapy.

twitter.title: First Session Lyrics - The Unseen Chorus
twitter.description: 'Maybe here in this room I will find my way' - from The Unseen Chorus (A Gay Musical)

essay: "First Session" captures the terrifying yet necessary moment of walking into therapy for the first time. The protagonist wrestles with doubt — "do I really need therapy?" — while the physical details of an oversized chair and too-wide room convey vulnerability and smallness in the face of healing.

The song's repeated question, "If I speak aloud will I breathe again?", drives home the musical's core belief: that breaking silence is the first act of survival. By the final lines, doubt gives way to the fragile, hopeful possibility of belonging.
song.lyricsHtml:
I know this is the next step for me
But do I really need therapy

A chair too big a room too wide
I want to run I want to hide
But still I sit though my hands are weak
Afraid of the truth in the words I speak

If I speak aloud will I breathe again
If I tell my truth will it ease the pain
Silence never saved me silence held me fast
Maybe words can heal me maybe this will last

I say I'm fine but the mask feels thin
A storm still rages deep within
The weight I drag has chained me down
Could this small room turn it around

If I speak aloud will I breathe again
If I trust these walls will I break the chain
I'm not asking for miracles not begging to be cured
Just a hand to steady me someone reassured

I miss who I was before I lost me

If I speak aloud I might breathe again
If I tell the truth I might feel again
Silence never saved me silence fades away
Maybe here in this room I will find my way

Maybe I belong here
Maybe I can start
